name: Les Coves de Vinromà and
name_original: La Plana Alta — Circular desde les Coves de Vinromá hasta el refugio de La Llobatera
description: This moderate 20.3-mile mountain biking route near Les Coves de Vinromà offers a scenic ride to the Refugio de La Llobatera.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Les Coves De Vinromà offers a diverse landscape for riders. The region features rolling hills and valleys, with elevations ranging from gentle climbs to more significant ascents. Trails often traverse agricultural lands, Mediterranean scrub, and forested areas. This varied terrain provides a range of experiences for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Les Coves De Vinromà The most popular mountain bike trail is Les Coves de Vinromà and, a 20.3 miles (32.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 50 minutes to complete. This route offers a moderate challenge through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is La Morería – Llobateres Junction loop from les Coves de Vinromà, an easy 8.2 miles (13.1 km) path. This circular route provides accessible riding through the immediate surroundings of Les Coves De Vinromà. Local mountain bikers also love the Mas de Pedra Seca loop from la Torre d'en Doménec, a 62.4 miles (100.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 9 hours 18 minutes. Mountain biking around Les Coves De Vinromà is defined by its mix of agricultural paths, forested sections, and open hillsides. The network offers options for easy mountain bike trails as well as more advanced mountain bike trails, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Les Coves De Vinromà are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 490 reviews. More than 2,400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Les Coves De Vinromà's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Les Coves De Vinromà?
A: Les Coves De Vinromà offers a wide network of over 150 mountain bike trails, catering to various skill levels. This includes approximately 25 easy, 90 moderate, and 35 difficult routes.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Les Coves De Vinromà?
A: Yes, there are approximately 25 easy mountain bike trails in the area. A good option for beginners is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14712346" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morería – Llobateres Junction loop from les Coves de Vinromà&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.2-mile (13.1 km) path that offers accessible riding through the immediate surroundings.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails for advanced riders?
A: For experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38251179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mas de Pedra Seca loop from la Torre d'en Doménec&lt;/a&gt;. This demanding 62.4-mile (100.5 km) trail features significant elevation gain and varied terrain, typically taking over 9 hours to complete. Another difficult option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19439983" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Tirig – Mas Blanch Ruins loop from Tírig&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 48.9 miles (78.6 km) with substantial climbing.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Les Coves De Vinromà are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14712346" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morería – Llobateres Junction loop from les Coves de Vinromà&lt;/a&gt; is an easy circular option, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3969736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Giant blue chair – La Pobla Tornesa loop from la Torre d'en Doménec&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 35.8-mile (57.5 km) circular ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Les Coves De Vinromà?
A: The Mediterranean climate of Les Coves De Vinromà makes spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) ideal for mountain biking. Temperatures are milder, and the landscape is often lush. Summer can be very hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended if visiting during July and August.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: The terrain around Les Coves De Vinromà is diverse, featuring a mix of agricultural paths, Mediterranean scrub, and forested sections. You'll encounter rolling hills and valleys, with elevations ranging from gentle climbs to more significant ascents, providing varied experiences for all skill levels.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Les Coves De Vinromà?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 490 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscape, the mix of agricultural paths and natural sections, and the variety of routes available for different abilities.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or natural attractions along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region offers several points of interest. You might encounter historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/244763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Xivert&lt;/a&gt; or natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1657526" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prat de Cabanes-Torreblanca Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;. Mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3410303" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de la Bandereta&lt;/a&gt; also offer scenic views.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the mountain bike trails in Les Coves De Vinromà?
A: Many trails in the region offer panoramic views, especially as you gain elevation. Look out for routes that traverse higher ground or pass through mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3984447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Serratella&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3923150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Portell&lt;/a&gt;, which are known for their scenic vistas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike trails in this region?
A: Trail durations vary significantly based on difficulty and distance. Easy routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14712346" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morería – Llobateres Junction loop&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in under 1.5 hours, while moderate trails such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931938779/les-coves-de-vinroma-and" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Coves de Vinromà and&lt;/a&gt; typically take around 2 hours 50 minutes. Longer, difficult routes can extend to over 9 hours.

Q: Are there options for refreshments or cafes along the mountain bike routes?
A: While many routes traverse rural and natural areas, the villages and towns within and around Les Coves De Vinromà, such as the town of Les Coves De Vinromà itself or nearby La Torre d'en Doménec, offer cafes and restaurants where you can refuel. Planning your route to pass through these settlements is advisable for refreshment stops.
tips: Access track to the spectacular refuge and through leafy pine forests. The towns of Les Coves de Vinromà and Villanova d'Alcolea can be linked, also giving access to the entire massos area.

Set of buildings in a farmhouse currently inhabited by few inhabitants. Place of passage on different routes through the Masos area.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is rated as moderate and requires good fitness and advanced riding skills. The technical difficulty is approximately STS S2, meaning it's best suited for experienced mountain bikers.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses a varied landscape in the La Plana Alta region. You'll encounter a mix of quiet asphalt roads and well-maintained dirt tracks, passing through agricultural areas with almond, carob, and olive trees, framed by modest mountain formations.

faqs.question: What points of interest will I encounter along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable spots. You'll pass by the La Llobatera Refuge, a rural house set in nature, and the Font del Mas de Calaf. You'll also go through the area of Mas de Carruano. The starting point, Les Coves de Vinromà, is also rich in history and natural beauty.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the La Plana Alta mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Les Coves de Vinromà. You should be able to find parking within the village to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The region generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking outside of the hottest summer months. Spring and autumn are typically ideal, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Always check local weather forecasts before you go.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local paths, including the Camí de Calaf, Camí de Torreblanca, Camí dels Bovalars, Camí de la Covantoll, Camí del Bustal, Camí del Pla de Mans, and Ruta dels Molins.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact route are not detailed, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near settlements, to ensure their safety and respect local wildlife and property.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that a permit or entrance fee is required to access this mountain bike trail. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like water or restrooms along the route?
faqs.answer: The La Llobatera Refuge is a rural house and might offer some basic amenities, but it's best to be self-sufficient. The village of Les Coves de Vinromà at the start/end point will have facilities. It's recommended to carry enough water and snacks for the entire duration of your ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and length, you should bring a well-maintained mountain bike, helmet, appropriate cycling attire, sufficient water and snacks, a repair kit, a first-aid kit, and a charged phone with the Komoot app for navigation. Sun protection is also advisable.

faqs.question: Are there any options for food or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The village of Les Coves de Vinromà, where the route begins and ends, offers options for both food and accommodation. You can find restaurants and places to stay there to complement your mountain biking experience.
